comments.text: This type of problem is one step above other arithmetical problems, like, say, Erdos Straus conjecture, in terms of difficulty. Because the latter at least can have at most countably many counterexampĺes, but here uncountably many exceptions could exist, making the prospect of eliminating every single one of them very ambitious.

Progress here may be linked to progress on Mahler's 3/2 problem. A key difficulty is that theoretically the integer parts $\lfloor C^k \rfloor$ could be almost all concentrated in a single residue class $b \hbox{ mod } W$, which would (morally at least) place an upper limit of $\phi(W)/W$ on the density. If this type of concentration happened for a lot of $W$, then one could conceivably end up with zero density overall. Excluding this type of scenario seems related to making more progress on the 3/2 problem.
